gridpoint     dist is_coast clc_dist0   gp_lat   gp_lon gp_ind sta_lat sta_lon sta_id station_type
1       891 11.82396        1         0 52.36235 4.632353    167  52.318    4.79    240      primary
The raw dataset comprises 38 discrete point locations, each corresponding to (and co-located with) a KNMI station rather than a node on a regular latitude–longitude grid. Consequently, the points do not provide continuous spatial coverage, and inter-point spacing is irregular, reflecting the underlying station network rather than a uniform grid structure.

```{r}
library(dplyr)              # 
library(ggplot2)            #
library(sf)                 # 
library(rnaturalearth)      # ne_countries()
#install.packages("rnaturalearthhires", epos = "https://ropensci.r-universe.dev")
library(rnaturalearthhires) # scale = "large" 
library(ggrepel)            # geom_text_repel, geom_label_repel
library(here)  

# Function 1: Basic Station Map with Temperature/WBGT Distribution
plot_station_temperature_map <- function(data, 
                                        city_name = NULL,
                                        time_point = NULL, 
                                        variable = "temperature",
                                        include_wow = TRUE) {
  
  # Select time point
  if(is.null(time_point)) {
    plot_data <- data %>%
      filter(!is.na(WBGT_C)) %>%
      slice(1)
  } else {
    plot_data <- data %>%
      filter(Valid_datetime == time_point)
  }
  
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    stop("No data available for the specified time point")
  }
  
  # Auto-detect city name if not provided
  if(is.null(city_name)) {
    city_name <- plot_data$nearest_city[1]
  }
  
  # Get Netherlands map
  netherlands <- ne_countries(scale = "large", country = "Netherlands", returnclass = "sf")
  
  # Initialize stations dataframe
  stations_df <- data.frame()
  
  # Select which variable to plot
  if(variable == "temperature") {
    knmi_value <- plot_data$T_DRYB_10[1]
    grid_value <- plot_data$Temperature_C[1]
    color_label <- "Temperature (°C)"
    midpoint_val <- 15
  } else {
    knmi_value <- plot_data$WBGT_obs_10[1]
    grid_value <- plot_data$WBGT_C[1]
    color_label <- "WBGT (°C)"
    midpoint_val <- 20
  }
  
  # Debug: Print KNMI value
  if(is.na(knmi_value)) {
    cat("Note: KNMI", ifelse(variable == "temperature", "temperature", "WBGT"), 
        "value is NA for station", plot_data$station_id[1], "\n")
  }
  
  # Add city center
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$nearest_city_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$nearest_city_lat[1],
    type = "City Center",
    name = city_name,
    value = NA,
    distance_km = 0,
    data_source = "Reference"
  ))
  
  # Add KNMI station
  if(!is.na(knmi_value)) {
    stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
      lon = plot_data$station_lon[1],
      lat = plot_data$station_lat[1],
      type = "KNMI Station",
      name = paste("KNMI", plot_data$station_id[1]),
      value = knmi_value,
      distance_km = plot_data$nearest_city_distance_km[1],
      data_source = "Official"
    ))
  } else {
    stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
      lon = plot_data$station_lon[1],
      lat = plot_data$station_lat[1],
      type = "KNMI Station",
      name = paste("KNMI", plot_data$station_id[1]),
      value = NA,
      distance_km = plot_data$nearest_city_distance_km[1],
      data_source = "Official"
    ))
  }
  
  # Add gridpoint
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$gridpoint_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$gridpoint_lat[1],
    type = "Model Gridpoint",
    name = paste("Grid", plot_data$Gridpoint_ID[1]),
    value = grid_value,
    distance_km = plot_data$gridpoint_to_station_dist[1],
    data_source = "Model"
  ))
  
  # Add WOW stations if requested and available
  if(include_wow && !is.na(plot_data$wow_data_available[1]) && plot_data$wow_data_available[1] == TRUE) {
    for(i in 1:5) {
      wow_id_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_ExternalSiteId")
      wow_lat_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_Latitude")
      wow_lon_col <- paste0("wow_station_", i, "_Longitude")
      wow_val_col <- if(variable == "temperature") {
        paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_temp")
      } else {
        paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_wbgt")
      }
      
      if(wow_id_col %in% names(plot_data) && 
         !is.na(plot_data[[wow_id_col]][1]) && 
         !is.na(plot_data[[wow_val_col]][1])) {
        stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
          lon = plot_data[[wow_lon_col]][1],
          lat = plot_data[[wow_lat_col]][1],
          type = "WOW Station",
          name = paste0("WOW", i),
          value = plot_data[[wow_val_col]][1],
          distance_km = NA,
          data_source = "Citizen"
        ))
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate map bounds
  lon_range <- range(stations_df$lon, na.rm = TRUE)
  lat_range <- range(stations_df$lat, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Add buffer
  lon_buffer <- diff(lon_range) * 0.3
  lat_buffer <- diff(lat_range) * 0.3
  
  # Get city center coordinates for connections
  city_center <- stations_df[stations_df$type == "City Center", ]
  
  # Create the plot
  p <- ggplot() +
    # Netherlands background
    geom_sf(data = netherlands, fill = "lightgray", color = "darkgray", alpha = 0.5) +
    coord_sf(xlim = lon_range + c(-lon_buffer, lon_buffer),
             ylim = lat_range + c(-lat_buffer, lat_buffer)) +
    
    # Add connections to city center
    geom_segment(data = stations_df[stations_df$type != "City Center",],
                 aes(x = city_center$lon[1],
                     y = city_center$lat[1],
                     xend = lon, yend = lat),
                 color = "gray70", linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.3) +
    
    # Add stations
    geom_point(data = stations_df,
               aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
                   color = value, 
                   shape = type, 
                   size = type),
               stroke = 1.5) +
    
    # Add value labels with ggrepel
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = stations_df[!is.na(stations_df$value),],
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
          label = sprintf("%.1f°C", value)),
      size = 3, 
      fontface = "bold",
      box.padding = 0.5,
      point.padding = 0.3,
      segment.color = 'grey50',
      segment.size = 0.2,
      max.overlaps = Inf,
      seed = 42,  # For reproducible positioning
      nudge_y = 0.01
    ) +
    
    # Add station names with ggrepel
    ggrepel::geom_label_repel(
      data = stations_df,
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = name),
      size = 2.5, 
      alpha = 0.9,
      box.padding = 0.35,
      point.padding = 0.3,
      label.padding = 0.15,
      segment.color = 'grey70',
      segment.size = 0.2,
      max.overlaps = Inf,
      seed = 42,
      nudge_y = -0.015,
      fill = "white"
    ) +
    
    # Color scale
    scale_color_gradient2(
      low = "blue", mid = "yellow", high = "red",
      midpoint = midpoint_val,
      name = color_label,
      na.value = "gray50",
      limits = c(min(stations_df$value, na.rm = TRUE) - 2,
                 max(stations_df$value, na.rm = TRUE) + 2)
    ) +
    
    # Shape and size scales
    scale_shape_manual(
      values = c("City Center" = 15, 
                 "KNMI Station" = 17, 
                 "Model Gridpoint" = 16,
                 "WOW Station" = 19),
      name = "Station Type"
    ) +
    scale_size_manual(
      values = c("City Center" = 6, 
                 "KNMI Station" = 5, 
                 "Model Gridpoint" = 4,
                 "WOW Station" = 3.5),
      guide = "none"
    ) +
    
    # Labels and theme
    labs(
      title = sprintf("%s Distribution across %s Weather Stations", 
                     ifelse(variable == "temperature", "Temperature", "WBGT"), 
                     city_name),
      subtitle = sprintf("Time: %s | WOW Coverage: %.0f%% | Quality: %s",
                        format(plot_data$Valid_datetime[1], "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"),
                        ifelse(is.na(plot_data$wow_coverage[1]), 0, plot_data$wow_coverage[1] * 100),
                        ifelse(is.na(plot_data$wow_data_quality[1]), "N/A", plot_data$wow_data_quality[1])),
      x = "Longitude",
      y = "Latitude",
      caption = "Data sources: KNMI official stations, ECMWF model gridpoints, WOW citizen stations"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "right",
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10)
    )
  
  return(p)
}
# Function 2: Simple Heat Map without interpolation
plot_temperature_heatmap_simple <- function(data, 
                                           city_name = NULL,
                                           time_point = NULL,
                                           variable = "wbgt") {
  
  # Select time point
  if(is.null(time_point)) {
    plot_data <- data %>%
      filter(!is.na(wow_data_available) & wow_data_available == TRUE) %>%
      slice(1)
  } else {
    plot_data <- data %>%
      filter(Valid_datetime == time_point)
  }
  
  if(nrow(plot_data) == 0) {
    warning("No data with WOW stations available for the specified time point")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Auto-detect city name
  if(is.null(city_name)) {
    city_name <- plot_data$nearest_city[1]
  }
  
  # Collect all station data
  stations_df <- data.frame()
  
  # Select variable
  if(variable == "temperature") {
    knmi_val <- plot_data$T_DRYB_10[1]
    grid_val <- plot_data$Temperature_C[1]
    var_label <- "Temperature (°C)"
  } else {
    knmi_val <- plot_data$WBGT_obs_10[1]
    grid_val <- plot_data$WBGT_C[1]
    var_label <- "WBGT (°C)"
  }
  
  # Add city center
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$nearest_city_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$nearest_city_lat[1],
    value = NA,
    type = "City Center",
    label = city_name
  ))
  
  # Add KNMI station
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$station_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$station_lat[1],
    value = knmi_val,
    type = "KNMI",
    label = plot_data$station_id[1]
  ))
  
  # Add gridpoint
  stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
    lon = plot_data$gridpoint_lon[1],
    lat = plot_data$gridpoint_lat[1],
    value = grid_val,
    type = "Model",
    label = paste("Grid", plot_data$Gridpoint_ID[1])
  ))
  
  # Add WOW stations
  for(i in 1:5) {
    wow_lon <- plot_data[[paste0("wow_station_", i, "_Longitude")]][1]
    wow_lat <- plot_data[[paste0("wow_station_", i, "_Latitude")]][1]
    wow_val <- if(variable == "temperature") {
      plot_data[[paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_temp")]][1]
    } else {
      plot_data[[paste0("wow_station_", i, "_station_wbgt")]][1]
    }
    
    if(!is.na(wow_lon) && !is.na(wow_lat) && !is.na(wow_val)) {
      stations_df <- rbind(stations_df, data.frame(
        lon = wow_lon,
        lat = wow_lat,
        value = wow_val,
        type = "WOW",
        label = paste0("WOW", i)
      ))
    }
  }
  
  # Calculate bounds
  lon_range <- range(stations_df$lon, na.rm = TRUE)
  lat_range <- range(stations_df$lat, na.rm = TRUE)
  lon_buffer <- diff(lon_range) * 0.2
  lat_buffer <- diff(lat_range) * 0.2
  
  # Create plot with better text positioning
  p <- ggplot(stations_df) +
    # Add points with size proportional to value
    geom_point(data = stations_df[!is.na(stations_df$value),],
               aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
                   color = value, 
                   size = value),
               alpha = 0.7) +
    
    # Add city center
    geom_point(data = stations_df[stations_df$type == "City Center",],
               aes(x = lon, y = lat),
               color = "red", shape = 18, size = 5) +
    
    # Use ggrepel for station labels
    ggrepel::geom_label_repel(
      data = stations_df,
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = label),
      size = 2.5,
      box.padding = 0.35,
      point.padding = 0.3,
      segment.color = 'grey50',
      segment.size = 0.2,
      max.overlaps = Inf,
      alpha = 0.9,
      seed = 42,
      fill = "white"
    ) +
    
    # Use ggrepel for value labels
    ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
      data = stations_df[!is.na(stations_df$value),],
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, 
          label = sprintf("%.1f°C", value)),
      size = 3, 
      fontface = "bold",
      box.padding = 0.25,
      point.padding = 0.25,
      segment.color = 'grey30',
      segment.size = 0.2,
      max.overlaps = Inf,
      seed = 42
    ) +
    
    # Color scale
    scale_color_gradient2(
      low = "blue", mid = "yellow", high = "red",
      midpoint = ifelse(variable == "temperature", 15, 20),
      name = var_label
    ) +
    
    scale_size_continuous(
      range = c(5, 15),
      guide = "none"
    ) +
    
    # Labels and theme
    labs(
      title = sprintf("%s Distribution - %s", var_label, city_name),
      subtitle = sprintf("Time: %s | %d stations reporting",
                        format(plot_data$Valid_datetime[1], "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M"),
                        sum(!is.na(stations_df$value))),
      x = "Longitude",
      y = "Latitude",
      caption = "Red diamond: city center | Circle size: value magnitude"
    ) +
    coord_fixed(ratio = 1.3) +
    xlim(lon_range[1] - lon_buffer, lon_range[2] + lon_buffer) +
    ylim(lat_range[1] - lat_buffer, lat_range[2] + lat_buffer) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14)
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# Function 3: Time Series Comparison Plot (保持不变)
plot_station_timeseries <- function(data, 
                                   city_name = NULL,
                                   hours_to_plot = 24) {
  
  # Auto-detect city name
  if(is.null(city_name)) {
    city_name <- unique(data$nearest_city)[1]
  }
  
  # Filter to recent hours
  latest_time <- max(data$Valid_datetime, na.rm = TRUE)
  start_time <- latest_time - (hours_to_plot * 3600)
  
  plot_data <- data %>%
    filter(Valid_datetime >= start_time & Valid_datetime <= latest_time)
  
  # Prepare long format data for plotting
  time_series_data <- plot_data %>%
    select(Valid_datetime, 
           KNMI = WBGT_obs_10,
           Model = WBGT_C,
           WOW_Mean = wow_wbgt_mean,
           WOW_Max = wow_wbgt_max) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = -Valid_datetime,
                names_to = "Source",
                values_to = "WBGT")
  
  # Create time series plot
  p <- ggplot(time_series_data, aes(x = Valid_datetime, y = WBGT)) +
    geom_line(aes(color = Source), linewidth = 1.2, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_point(aes(color = Source, shape = Source), size = 2, na.rm = TRUE) +
    
    scale_color_manual(values = c("KNMI" = "darkgreen",
                                  "Model" = "blue",
                                  "WOW_Mean" = "purple",
                                  "WOW_Max" = "red")) +
    
    labs(title = sprintf("WBGT Time Series Comparison - %s", city_name),
         subtitle = sprintf("Last %d hours | Mean WOW stations: %.1f",
                           hours_to_plot,
                           mean(plot_data$wow_n_stations, na.rm = TRUE)),
         x = "Time",
         y = "WBGT (°C)",
         color = "Data Source",
         shape = "Data Source") +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom",
          axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  
  # Add uncertainty band if data is available
  if(all(c("wow_wbgt_min", "wow_wbgt_max") %in% names(plot_data))) {
    ribbon_data <- plot_data %>%
      filter(!is.na(wow_wbgt_min) & !is.na(wow_wbgt_max))
    
    if(nrow(ribbon_data) > 0) {
      p <- p + geom_ribbon(data = ribbon_data,
                          aes(x = Valid_datetime,
                              ymin = wow_wbgt_min,
                              ymax = wow_wbgt_max),
                          alpha = 0.2, fill = "purple",
                          inherit.aes = FALSE)
    }
  }
  
  return(p)
}
# Wrapper function to create all maps for a city
create_city_analysis_maps <- function(data, city_name = NULL, time_point = NULL) {
  
  # Create a list to store all plots
  plots <- list()
  
  # Temperature map
  plots$temp_map <- plot_station_temperature_map(
    data, 
    city_name = city_name,
    time_point = time_point,
    variable = "temperature",
    include_wow = TRUE
  )
  
  # WBGT map
  plots$wbgt_map <- plot_station_temperature_map(
    data, 
    city_name = city_name,
    time_point = time_point,
    variable = "wbgt",
    include_wow = TRUE
  )
  
  # Simple heat map
  plots$heatmap <- plot_temperature_heatmap_simple(
    data,
    city_name = city_name,
    time_point = time_point,
    variable = "wbgt"
  )
  
  # Time series
  plots$timeseries <- plot_station_timeseries(
    data,
    city_name = city_name,
    hours_to_plot = 24
  )
  
  return(plots)
}


data_amsterdam <- readRDS(here("Visualisation_and_its_data/Visualisation_data", "data_amsterdam_processed.rds"))

amsterdam_maps <- create_city_analysis_maps(
  data_amsterdam,
  city_name = "Amsterdam",
  time_point = as.POSIXct("2022-07-15 12:00:00", tz = "UTC")### Hi kate, here is the place to adjust time
)


# shows map
print(amsterdam_maps$temp_map)
print(amsterdam_maps$wbgt_map)
print(amsterdam_maps$heatmap)
```
